name: Multi-Stage Build Optimizer
description: Reduce Docker image size dramatically by using multi-stage builds to separate build dependencies from the runtime image.
trendingReason: Smaller images mean faster deploys and reduced attack surface

steps.title: Check current image size
steps.description: See how large the existing image is before optimization.

steps.title: Build with target stage for development
steps.description: Build only up to a specific stage in a multi-stage Dockerfile.
steps.tip: Use --target to build intermediate stages for debugging or development without running the full build.

steps.title: Build the production image
steps.description: Build the final slim production image with only runtime dependencies.

steps.title: Compare image sizes
steps.description: Verify the size reduction between development and production images.

steps.title: Inspect image layers
steps.description: See what each layer contributes to the image size.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Multi-Stage Build Optimizer
set -euo pipefail

IMAGE_NAME=${1:?"Usage: $0 <image-name>"}

echo "=== Current Image Size ==="
docker images "$IMAGE_NAME" --format 'table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.Size}}' 2>/dev/null || echo "Image not found"

echo ""
echo "=== Building production image ==="
docker build -t "$IMAGE_NAME:optimized" .

echo ""
echo "=== Image Comparison ==="
docker images "$IMAGE_NAME" --format 'table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.Size}}'

echo ""
echo "=== Layer Analysis ==="
docker history "$IMAGE_NAME:optimized" --format 'table {{.Size}}\t{{.CreatedBy}}' | head -15

echo ""
echo "=== Optimization Tips ==="
echo "1. Use alpine or distroless as final base"
echo "2. Combine RUN commands to reduce layers"
echo "3. Order Dockerfile commands by change frequency (least to most)"
echo "4. Use .dockerignore to exclude unnecessary files"
echo "5. Copy only required artifacts from builder stage"

faq.question: What base image should I use for the final stage?
faq.answer: Alpine Linux (5MB) for most cases, or Google's distroless images for maximum security. For Go apps, you can even use 'scratch' (0MB).

faq.question: Does multi-stage increase build time?
faq.answer: Build time may increase slightly because of additional stages, but Docker caches each stage independently. Subsequent builds are fast if earlier stages haven't changed.
